title: Kit Digital 2026: The Complete Guide for Spanish Businesses (€3,000–€29,000 in EU Grants)
description: A plain-English guide to Spain's Kit Digital 2026 for freelancers and SMEs: what it is, how much you get per segment (€3,000–€29,000), the requirements, and how to apply for the digital voucher.
dek: The EU is subsidising the digitalisation of Spanish businesses through the Kit Digital voucher. Here is the plain-English version: how much you get, who qualifies, and how to be ready so you don't miss out.
author: The Kodable team
takeaways: Kit Digital is an EU grant (NextGenerationEU): it gives you a voucher to digitalise your business, and you never pay it back.
The amount ranges from €3,000 (freelancers) to €29,000 (companies up to 250 employees), depending on your segment.
Applying is four steps: register on Acelera Pyme, do the self-assessment test, submit the application, and choose a digitiser.
In 2026 the programme runs until the funds run out, get ready now, because it is handed out first-come, first-served.

body.alt: Official emblem: Funded by the European Union, NextGenerationEU
body.caption: Kit Digital is financed by the European Union's NextGenerationEU funds.

body.text: The European Union is putting money on the table so that Spanish businesses go digital. It is called **Kit Digital**, and since 2022 it has handed out billions in vouchers for websites, online shops, cybersecurity and more. Most of the freelancers and small businesses that could claim it... simply don't. This guide explains, without the jargon, what it is, how much you get, and how to be ready so you are not left out.
What Kit Digital actually is, in plain terms

Kit Digital is a public grant programme from the Government of Spain, managed by **Red.es** and financed by the European [NextGenerationEU](https://espanadigital.gob.es/lineas-de-actuacion/programa-kit-digital) funds under the Recovery Plan. Translated: the administration gives you a **digital voucher** (a fixed amount of money) so you can hire digital solutions (a website, an online shop, a CRM, cybersecurity) from approved companies called **agentes digitalizadores** (digitisers). You never touch the cash: the voucher pays the solution's invoice directly.
It is not a loan. You do not pay it back. It is a subsidy, conditional on you genuinely digitalising your business.
How much you get: amounts by segment
What you can receive depends on the size of your workforce. The programme splits beneficiaries into **segments**:

body.alt: Chart of Kit Digital voucher amounts by company segment, from €3,000 to €29,000
body.caption: Maximum voucher by headcount, from Segment III (freelancers) to Segment V (up to 250 employees).

items: **Segment III** (0–2 employees, most freelancers): up to **€3,000**.
**Segment II** (3–9 employees): up to **€6,000**.
**Segment I** (10–49 employees): up to **€12,000**.
**Segment IV** (50–99 employees): up to **€25,000**.
**Segment V** (100–249 employees): up to **€29,000**.

body.label: the maximum voucher, for medium-sized companies of up to 250 employees (Segment V)

body.text: The important shift of the last couple of years? The programme **raised the Segment III voucher to €3,000** and **widened its reach to companies of up to 250 employees** (Segments IV and V), which used to be reserved for the smallest businesses. More and more companies now qualify.
Can you claim it? The basic requirements
Kit Digital is aimed at freelancers, micro-businesses and SMEs with a tax address in Spain. Broadly, you need to:

items: Be a **registered autónomo** or an SME/micro-business with fewer than 250 employees.
Have a **minimum trading history** (usually 6 months registered in the RETA or as a company).
Be **up to date** with your tax (Hacienda) and Social Security obligations.
Not exceed the accumulated **de minimis aid** limits of recent years.
Complete the **self-assessment test** (it is mandatory).

body.title: An honest note
body.body: The exact requirements (trading history, de minimis amounts, deadlines) are set by each call (convocatoria) in its own rules. Before you take anything for granted, confirm it in the [official rules](https://www.boe.es/buscar/doc.php?id=BOE-A-2026-2070) and on [acelerapyme.gob.es](https://www.acelerapyme.gob.es/kit-digital). We give you the map; the fine print always lives at the official source.

body.text: How to apply, step by step

body.alt: The four steps to apply for Kit Digital: register, self-assessment, application, digitiser
body.caption: The process end to end: registration, self-assessment, application, and choosing your digitiser.

steps.title: Register on Acelera Pyme
steps.body: Go to acelerapyme.gob.es and create your account with a digital certificate or Cl@ve. It is the front door to the whole process.

steps.title: Take the self-assessment test
steps.body: A short questionnaire (around 13 questions) that measures your digital maturity. There is no pass mark (you just have to do it) but it is mandatory in order to claim the voucher.

steps.title: Apply for your digital voucher
steps.body: When your call is open, you submit the application on the Red.es electronic office, in the procedure for your segment.

steps.title: Choose your digitiser
steps.body: Once the voucher is granted, you pick a company from the official catalogue of agentes digitalizadores, who delivers the solution and invoices against your voucher.

body.text: What can you spend the voucher on?
The voucher is not free cash: it is spent on **categories of solutions** from the official catalogue. The most useful ones for a local business:

body.alt: Categories of digital solutions funded by Kit Digital, including website, e-commerce, CRM, cybersecurity and AI
body.caption: Some of the eligible solutions from the official catalogue.

items: **Website and online presence**: a professional site so people find you on Google.
**E-commerce**: selling online, with a catalogue and a payment gateway.
**Social media** and **marketing** management to win customers.
**Customer management (CRM)** so no opportunity slips through.
**Cybersecurity** and **secure workplace** to protect your data.
**E-invoicing**, **virtual office** and **process management** to save hours every week.

body.text: And the direction of travel is clear: the programme keeps adding **artificial intelligence** and managed-cybersecurity solutions, exactly where a small business gains the most time.
The 2026 status: is it open right now?

Here we have to be straight, because there is a lot of confusing information out there. The original Kit Digital calls (Segments I, II and III) **closed in October 2025**. In January 2026, the official gazette published [Orden TDF/39/2026](https://www.boe.es/buscar/doc.php?id=BOE-A-2026-2070), which **reactivates the programme's legal framework** and allows the **leftover European funds** from earlier calls to be redistributed.

What does that mean for you today? That the programme **drops the rigid closing date** and instead runs **until the funds are exhausted**. New calls are published as those remaining funds get reassigned. Which is why the real status changes from week to week: the only reliable answer is the official source.

body.title: Where to check the real status
body.body: Always look at the Red.es electronic office, [sede.red.gob.es](https://sede.red.gob.es), and [acelerapyme.gob.es](https://www.acelerapyme.gob.es/kit-digital) before acting. If your call is open, the deadline is shown there; if not, it appears as closed or coming soon.

body.text: Why get ready TODAY, even if your call is not open

There is a lesson from the previous calls worth burning into memory: **the money is handed out in order of arrival, until it runs out.** The businesses that already had their registration done, their self-assessment passed and their digitiser chosen were able to apply the moment the window opened. The ones who started finding out that day arrived too late.

items: Create your account and **do the self-assessment now**: it is free and does not expire.
Decide **which solutions** you want to fund (website, online shop, CRM, cybersecurity).
Have **your digitiser identified** so you are not improvising on opening day.

body.text: The voucher does not go to whoever needs it most, but to whoever shows up ready on the day it opens.
How Kodable helps

We build exactly the kind of solutions Kit Digital subsidises: **websites that sell, online shops, AI agents, automations and custom tools** for local businesses. We help you reach opening day with everything ready, the registration, the self-assessment, and a clear plan for what to build with your voucher.

An honest note on the fine print: for the voucher to pay the invoice, the solution must be delivered by an **agente digitalizador from the official catalogue**. We walk the whole path with you (design, build and launch) and guide you to the right digitiser when the moment comes. No promises we can't keep.

body.title: Get ready for your Kit Digital
body.body: Tell us about your business in a 15-minute call. We'll tell you what you can fund and leave your application ready for when your call opens.

body.text: Frequently asked questions
Do you have to pay Kit Digital back?
No. It is a subsidy, not a loan. If you meet the conditions and justify the solution, there is nothing to repay.
How much can a freelancer with no employees claim?
A freelancer in Segment III (0–2 employees) can receive up to **€3,000** in digital voucher.
Can I choose any company to build my website?
Only if it is registered as an **agente digitalizador** in the official Red.es catalogue. The funded solution must be delivered by an approved digitiser.
Is the application window open right now?

It depends on the call and the moment. The framework has been reactivated since 2026 and runs until funds are exhausted; check the status on [sede.red.gob.es](https://sede.red.gob.es) and [acelerapyme.gob.es](https://www.acelerapyme.gob.es/kit-digital).

title: Getting Found When AI Answers First: A 2026 Guide for Local Businesses
description: Google now writes an AI answer at the top of the page, so fewer searches end in a click. Here is what AI Overviews and AI Mode change for a local business, and a plain-English playbook to still get found: clear answers, reviews, Google Business Profile and speed.
dek: Google increasingly writes the answer at the top of the page, and fewer people click through. Here is what actually changed for a local business, and the honest playbook for getting found when AI answers first.
author: The Kodable team
takeaways: Google increasingly answers the question itself, in an AI box at the top of the page. When that box appears, clicks to websites roughly halve, and most searches already end with no click at all.
The customer-rich searches for a local business, near me, open now, best X in your town, and Google Maps, are still very much alive. AI has not replaced them.
The goal shifted from ranking number one to being the clear, trustworthy answer the AI quotes. People call it GEO or AEO, and being cited is now a different game from ranking.

What actually works is unglamorous and evidence-backed: answer real questions clearly, back it with real detail, own your Google Business Profile and reviews, earn honest mentions, and keep the site fast.

cta.label: Get a site AI can recommend

body.alt: A Google results page in 2026: an AI-written answer sits at the top, above the familiar list of website links
body.caption: The results page in 2026: Google writes an answer at the top, and the familiar list of links starts further down.

body.text: Type a question into Google today and, more and more often, you do not get a tidy list of ten blue links. You get an **answer**, written by AI, sitting at the very top of the page, with a handful of sources beside it. It is called an **AI Overview**, and it is quietly changing how customers find local businesses, faster than most owners have noticed.
What actually changed

Two things landed on Google in the last couple of years. **AI Overviews**: the AI-written summary that now appears above the normal results for many searches. And **AI Mode**: a full conversational way to search, where you ask follow-up questions like you would a person. Google is not alone either, people now ask **ChatGPT, Gemini and Perplexity** the kind of questions they used to type into a search bar. And yes, [this is already live in Spain, in Spanish](https://blog.google/feed/were-bringing-the-helpfulness-of-ai-overviews-to-more-countries-in-europe/).

body.label: people a month now use Google's AI Overviews, and AI Mode has passed 1 billion. This is simply how a lot of people search now.

body.text: The effect is simple: the answer often satisfies the person right there, on Google's page, without a click. Analysts call it a **zero-click search**, and it was already the norm before AI, [nearly 6 in 10 searches in the EU](https://sparktoro.com/blog/2024-zero-click-search-study-for-every-1000-us-google-searches-only-374-clicks-go-to-the-open-web-in-the-eu-its-360/) ended without a click to any website back in 2024. AI Overviews are pouring fuel on that fire.

body.label: people click a source inside a Google AI answer. And when that answer shows, clicks through to any website roughly halve, from 15% to 8% of searches.

body.text: So is this bad news for a small business?

Yes and no, and anyone who tells you only one half is selling something. The clicks AI Overviews absorb are mostly **informational**: the what-is and how-do-I questions, the general how-to articles. That traffic is genuinely down. But the searches that bring a local business actual customers are different: **near me, open now, book, best [what you do] in [your town]**. Those still lead to a phone call, a booking, a visit, and to **Google Maps**, which the AI box has not replaced.

body.title: An honest note
body.body: We are not going to pretend the old playbook is untouched. Clicks on general informational articles really have dropped. What still works, and works better than ever, is being genuinely the clearest, most trustworthy answer, and being visible where the AI looks. That is not a trick you buy; it is something you build.

body.label: of the businesses quoted in a Google AI answer come from the top-10 results, down from about 76% a year earlier. Being the answer is now a different game from ranking first.

body.text: The shift: from ranking to being the answer

For twenty years, getting found meant **ranking a page**, climbing to the top of the list. The new game, people call it **GEO** (generative engine optimization) or **AEO** (answer engine optimization), is about being the source the AI **quotes**. The goal has not really changed: be the business the machine, and the customer, decide to trust. The surface just moved.

body.alt: Four signals that push a business into an AI answer: clear answers, reviews and mentions, structured data, and a strong local presence
body.caption: What pushes a business into the answer: clear answers to real questions, reviews and mentions, a machine-readable site, and a strong local presence.

body.text: What to actually do (that works today)

There is no magic switch, and anyone selling one is guessing. But there is real evidence about what helps. The most striking finding: in a [peer-reviewed study](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.09735), pages that answered clearly and backed it up with **real statistics, quotes and cited sources** were up to 30 to 40 percent more visible in AI answers. Keyword-stuffing did nothing. In plain terms, the way to win is to genuinely be the best answer.

steps.title: Answer the real questions, clearly
steps.body: Write the plain answers to what customers actually ask: price ranges, opening hours, parking, whether you do X. AI answers are short and question-shaped, so a page that answers one real question in a few clear sentences is exactly the shape it quotes.

steps.title: Be genuinely worth quoting
steps.body: Back your answers with real specifics: an actual number, a real example, a named source. This is the one lever with hard evidence behind it. Thin, vague, spun-up text is precisely what the new systems filter out.

steps.title: Own your Google Business Profile
steps.body: For local searches, Maps and the local results still decide who gets the call. A complete profile, real photos, current hours and answered reviews do more for a local business than most technical SEO ever did.

steps.title: Earn mentions elsewhere
steps.body: AI cross-checks. Because being quoted no longer depends only on your own ranking, honest reviews, a listing in the right local directories and a mention in the local press all help the machine trust and repeat your name.

steps.title: Keep the basics right
steps.body: A fast, clean, well-structured site helps people and machines alike. Add structured data (schema) so Google can show rich results, but be honest about it: in the best test to date, schema on its own did not win more AI citations. It is hygiene, not a magic trick.

body.text: What to ignore

items: Anyone **guaranteeing number one on Google**, or now **number one in ChatGPT**. Nobody controls those rankings, and nobody can promise them.
**Keyword-stuffing** and thin, AI-spun filler articles, the exact thing the new systems are built to filter out.
**Buying reviews.** Against the rules, increasingly easy to detect, and a fast way to lose trust with customers and Google alike.

The claim that **schema markup alone gets you into AI answers.** Do it for rich results, but the [best test we have seen](https://ahrefs.com/blog/schema-ai-citations/) found it did not move AI citations by itself.
Panic. The fundamentals, be findable, be trustworthy, be the best answer, did not change. Only the surface did.

body.text: AI did not kill getting found. It moved the finish line from ranked first to trusted enough to be quoted.
How Kodable helps

We build sites made for exactly this: fast, cleanly structured, with your Google Business Profile and local signals set up, and the plain, genuinely useful answers your customers, and the AI, are looking for. No tricks, and no guarantees we cannot keep, just being the clearest, most trustworthy answer in your area, in a form both people and machines understand.

body.title: Get a site AI can find and recommend
body.body: Tell us about your business in a 15-minute call. We will show you where you are invisible to AI search today, and what is worth fixing first.

body.text: Frequently asked questions
Is SEO dead?
No, it changed. Being findable and trustworthy matters more than ever; the mechanics moved from ten blue links to AI answers and Maps. The work is different, not gone.
What is the difference between SEO, GEO and AEO?

SEO is ranking in the classic list of results. GEO and AEO are about being the source an AI answer quotes. In practice they overlap almost completely: the same clear, trustworthy, well-structured site tends to win all three at once.
Will an AI Overview actually mention my business?
It can, especially for local and specific questions, when your information is clear, consistent across the web, and genuinely useful. There is no guarantee, and anyone promising one is guessing.
Do I need to be on ChatGPT to get found?

You do not publish onto ChatGPT. These tools pull from the open web, your site, your Google profile, your reviews, your mentions. Get those right and you turn up in the answer, wherever the question is asked.
